title: ¡Viva! Delirium in Madrid as Spain reach World Cup final
summary: Roars of joy and honking car horns reverberated through Madrid on Tuesday as Spaniards celebrated their team overcoming France and moving to the cusp of a second World Cup crown.

title: Spain's Sánchez 'embarrassed' by ex-PM's 'no French players' remark
summary: Spanish leader Pedro Sánchez told French counterpart Sebastien Lecornu and first lady Brigitte Macron on Tuesday he was embarrassed by a predecessor's comments that their national football team had "no French players," media reported.

title: Spain registers hottest first half of summer on record
summary: Mainland Spain registered its hottest first half of the summer since records began in 1961, with two heatwaves sending temperatures soaring, national weather agency Aemet said on Tuesday.

title: 'Europe is back': New era for Gibraltar as border controls with Spain end
summary: A new era began between Spain and the tiny British territory of Gibraltar on Wednesday, as border checks that have long been a source of tension and frustration were finally lifted.

title: Seven Brits and one American among 12 foreigners killed in Spain's wildfire
summary: Twelve of the 13 victims of a deadly wildfire that swept through Andalusia in southern Spain were foreign nationals, the authorities said on Tuesday after completing post-mortem examinations.

title: Today in Spain: A roundup of the latest news on Wednesday
summary: Zaragoza and Valencia on red alert for 42C temperatures, 86-year-old Brit among injured at Spain's San Fermín bull runs, and more news on Wednesday July 15th.

title: Majestic Spain stun France to reach World Cup final
summary: Spain stunned tournament favourites France 2-0 to reach the World Cup final on Tuesday, producing a tactical masterpiece to shatter Les Bleus' dreams of winning the title for the third time.

title: Ticking time bomb? Europe's ageing population brings challenges
summary: The population of the 27-nation European Union will peak in 2029 before falling in the coming decades, according to a report published Tuesday that spotlights the major challenges the bloc faces from an ageing population.

title: EU lawmakers want to turn off addictive social media features for children
summary: There should be a "youth mode" for children on social media platforms in which addictive features and targeted advertising are turned off, EU lawmakers demanded on Tuesday.
